quiz.title: High-School Geometry: Unit 2 Quiz 9
quiz.subtitle: Theorems Regarding Lines That Intersect at 90°
quiz.subject: Geometry

questions.question: Two lines are perpendicular if and only if they intersect to form:
options: Two acute angles and two obtuse angles
Two supplementary angles of unequal measure
Four acute angles of equal measure
questions.explanation: Perpendicular lines intersect at $90^\circ$, forming four right angles. Since all right angles are congruent, all four angles are congruent and each measures $90^\circ$.

questions.question: If two lines are perpendicular, then they intersect at exactly one point. This point is called:
questions.explanation: Perpendicular lines intersect at exactly one point, which is simply called the point of intersection. The foot of a perpendicular refers specifically to the point where a perpendicular segment meets a line.

questions.question: If $\overleftrightarrow{AB} \perp \overleftrightarrow{CD}$ at point $E$, what is $m\angle AEC$?
questions.explanation: When two lines are perpendicular, all four angles formed at the intersection are right angles. Therefore, any angle formed by the intersecting lines, including $\angle AEC$, measures $90^\circ$.

questions.question: If a transversal is perpendicular to one of two parallel lines, what is its relationship to the other parallel line?
options: It is parallel to the other line
It is perpendicular to the other line
questions.explanation: The Perpendicular Transversal Theorem states that if a transversal is perpendicular to one of two parallel lines, then it is perpendicular to the other parallel line as well.

questions.question: Two lines are both perpendicular to the same line. What is the relationship between these two lines?
options: They are perpendicular to each other
They are parallel to each other
questions.explanation: If two lines are both perpendicular to the same line, then they are parallel to each other. This follows from the fact that if corresponding angles are congruent (both $90^\circ$), the lines must be parallel.

questions.question: Line $\ell$ is perpendicular to line $m$ at point $P$. Which statement must be true?
options: $P$ is the midpoint of the segment where the lines intersect
All four angles formed at $P$ measure $90^\circ$
The two lines lie in different planes
questions.explanation: Perpendicular lines intersect to form four right angles. All four angles at the intersection point $P$ measure exactly $90^\circ$ by definition of perpendicular lines.

questions.question: In a plane, how many lines can be drawn perpendicular to a given line through a point on that line?
questions.explanation: Through a given point on a line, exactly one perpendicular line can be drawn in a plane. This is guaranteed by the Perpendicular Postulate.

questions.question: In a plane, how many lines can be drawn perpendicular to a given line through a point not on that line?
questions.explanation: Through a point not on a given line, exactly one perpendicular line can be drawn to that line in a plane. This is also guaranteed by the Perpendicular Postulate.

questions.question: If $\overleftrightarrow{AB} \perp \overleftrightarrow{CD}$, which notation correctly expresses this relationship?
questions.explanation: The symbol $\perp$ denotes perpendicularity. $\overleftrightarrow{AB} \perp \overleftrightarrow{CD}$ means line $AB$ is perpendicular to line $CD$.

questions.question: Line $p$ is perpendicular to line $q$. Line $r$ is parallel to line $q$. What is the relationship between line $p$ and line $r$?
options: Line $p$ and line $r$ intersect at a $45^\circ$ angle
questions.explanation: If $p \perp q$ and $q \parallel r$, then $p \perp r$. Since $r$ has the same direction as $q$, a line perpendicular to $q$ is also perpendicular to $r$. This follows from the corresponding angles all being $90^\circ$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You have mastered the theorems about perpendicular lines and their relationships to parallel lines.
scoreMessages.70: Good work! You understand the basics but review the Perpendicular Transversal Theorem and the fact that two lines perpendicular to the same line are parallel.
scoreMessages.50: A fair start. Focus on: perpendicular lines form four right angles, the Perpendicular Postulate guarantees exactly one perpendicular through any point, and perpendicularity interacts with parallelism in specific ways.
scoreMessages.0: Keep practicing! Perpendicular lines intersect at $90^\circ$. If a transversal is perpendicular to one parallel line, it is perpendicular to both. Two lines perpendicular to the same line are parallel to each other.
